museums.name: ARKEN Museum for Moderne Kunst
museums.description: ARKEN Museum of Modern Art is one of Denmark's leading contemporary art museums, housed in a dramatic ship-shaped building by the sea in Ishøj. The collection focuses on international and Danish art from the 1990s to the present. The architecture itself is a striking work of art.

museums.name: ARoS Aarhus Kunstmuseum
museums.description: ARoS Aarhus Art Museum is one of the largest art museums in northern Europe, renowned for its circular rainbow panorama walkway on the rooftop. The collection spans centuries of Danish and international art, from the Golden Age to cutting-edge contemporary works. A must-see landmark in Aarhus.

museums.name: Arbejdermuseet
museums.description: Arbejdermuseet tells the story of the Danish working class from the 1850s to the present day. Housed in a historic building in central Copenhagen, it features reconstructed interiors, personal stories, and everyday objects. A moving tribute to ordinary people's lives and the labour movement.

museums.name: Billund Kommunes Museer
museums.description: Billund Kommunes Museer preserves the cultural history of the Billund area, home to the invention of LEGO. Exhibitions explore the region's transformation from rural farming community to an internationally known destination. An engaging look at how one small town changed the world of play.

museums.name: Bornholms Automobilmuseum
museums.description: Bornholms Automobilmuseum in Aakirkeby celebrates the island's motoring heritage with a collection of vintage cars, motorcycles, and related memorabilia. The museum reflects Bornholm's connection to the wider history of personal transport. A charming specialist museum on Denmark's sunshine island.

museums.name: Bornholms Kunstmuseum
museums.description: Bornholms Kunstmuseum is dramatically situated on the rocky north coast of Bornholm, with panoramic views of the sea. The collection focuses on the rich tradition of Bornholm painters, who were drawn to the island's unique light. The building itself is beautifully integrated into the natural landscape.

museums.name: Bornholms Museum
museums.description: Bornholms Museum documents the island's history from the Stone Age to modern times. Exhibits cover everything from local crafts and trades to the German occupation during World War II. A comprehensive journey through life on Denmark's sunshine island.

museums.name: Brandts
museums.description: Brandts in Odense is a dynamic cultural centre occupying a former textile factory. It presents contemporary art, photography, and visual culture in a series of flexible exhibition spaces. One of Denmark's most innovative venues for modern visual arts.

museums.name: CLAY – Museum for Keramisk Kunst
museums.description: CLAY – Museum for Keramisk Kunst in Middelfart is a beautifully designed museum dedicated to ceramic art from Denmark and around the world. The collection spans historic studio pottery to cutting-edge contemporary ceramics. A specialist museum that celebrates one of the world's oldest art forms.

museums.name: Classic Car House
museums.description: Classic Car House near Lyngby is a private museum with an impressive collection of classic and vintage automobiles. The exhibition spans the history of the car from early motoring to mid-century classics. A paradise for automotive enthusiasts.

museums.name: Copenhagen Contemporary
museums.description: Copenhagen Contemporary is a major international contemporary art centre housed in a vast former industrial building on the Copenhagen waterfront. Its enormous exhibition spaces allow for monumental installations and ambitious international shows. A powerful new addition to the Nordic contemporary art scene.

museums.name: Danmarks Industrimuseum
museums.description: Danmarks Industrimuseum chronicles the industrial revolution and its profound impact on Danish society and everyday life. Located in a historic factory in Horsens, it features working machines, reconstructed workshops, and interactive exhibits. A fascinating exploration of the forces that shaped modern Denmark.

museums.name: Danmarks Jernbanemuseum
museums.description: Danmarks Jernbanemuseum in Odense is the national railway museum of Denmark. The vast collection of locomotives, carriages, and railway artefacts traces the history of Danish rail travel from the 19th century to the present. A must-visit for railway enthusiasts and families alike.

museums.name: Danmarks Tekniske Museum
museums.description: Danmarks Tekniske Museum in Helsingør is the national museum of science and technology. From early steam engines and vintage cars to pioneering aircraft and computers, it traces Denmark's technological journey. A hands-on museum that appeals to all ages.

museums.name: Dansk Jødisk Museum
museums.description: Dansk Jødisk Museum tells the story of Jewish life in Denmark over more than 400 years. Designed by architect Daniel Libeskind, the building's fragmented geometry reflects the complexity of Jewish-Danish history. A deeply moving and architecturally remarkable experience.

museums.name: Davids Samling
museums.description: Davids Samling in Copenhagen holds one of the world's finest collections of Islamic art, alongside European decorative arts. Founded by lawyer and collector C.L. David, the museum occupies a beautiful historic building in central Copenhagen. A remarkable and underrated collection of international importance.

museums.name: De Kulturhistoriske Museer i Holstebro Kommune
museums.description: De Kulturhistoriske Museer i Holstebro Kommune manage several museums across the Holstebro area, covering local and regional history. Collections include archaeological finds, historical interiors, and stories of everyday life in west Jutland. A rich window into this corner of Denmark's past.

museums.name: Den Frie Udstillingsbygning
museums.description: Den Frie Udstillingsbygning in Copenhagen is one of Denmark's oldest independent exhibition venues, built by artists in 1898 to show work outside the Royal Academy. The distinctive art nouveau building continues to present challenging and independent contemporary art. A historic institution with an enduring commitment to artistic freedom.

museums.name: Den Gamle By
museums.description: Den Gamle By in Aarhus is an open-air museum and living history town, featuring over 75 historic buildings from across Denmark. Costumed staff recreate everyday life in different eras, from the 1700s to the 1970s. One of Denmark's most beloved and visited cultural experiences.

museums.name: Den Kongelige Samling
museums.description: Den Kongelige Samling encompasses the royal palaces and their historic collections across Denmark. Visitors can explore the magnificent state rooms, royal art, and centuries of Danish royal history. A unique insight into the monarchy that has shaped the nation.

museums.name: Designmuseum Danmark
museums.description: Designmuseum Danmark in Copenhagen is dedicated to Danish and international design, craft, and applied arts. From iconic mid-century furniture to cutting-edge industrial design, it celebrates the creativity that made 'Danish design' a global benchmark. Housed in a beautiful 18th-century hospital building.

museums.name: Djurslands Jernbanemuseum
museums.description: Djurslands Jernbanemuseum in Ryomgård celebrates the history of the narrow-gauge railways that once served the Djursland peninsula. Visitors can ride on restored vintage trains through the countryside on selected days. A charming and nostalgic railway heritage experience.

museums.name: Egholm Museum
museums.description: Egholm Museum in Kirke-Hyllinge is a local history museum with collections covering the history of the area around Isefjord on Zealand. It preserves artefacts and stories from the farming and fishing communities that shaped this corner of rural Denmark. A welcoming community museum.

museums.name: Energimuseet
museums.description: Energimuseet explores Denmark's energy history, from the first windmills and watermills to modern renewable energy. Located at a historic power station in Bjerringbro, it examines how energy has transformed society. A timely museum for understanding Denmark's green transition.

museums.name: Esbjerg Kunstmuseum
museums.description: Esbjerg Kunstmuseum holds one of Denmark's finest collections of Danish art from the late 19th century to the present. The museum is particularly strong in works by the Skagen painters and modern Danish artists. A bright, well-curated museum in the heart of Denmark's largest North Sea port.

museums.name: Experimentarium
museums.description: Experimentarium in Hellerup is Denmark's leading science centre, offering hundreds of interactive experiments and exhibits exploring science, technology, and the human body. Housed in a spectacular new building, it is one of the most visited attractions in the Copenhagen area. A wonderful destination for families and curious minds.

museums.name: Faaborg Museum
museums.description: Faaborg Museum holds a significant collection of the Funen painters, a group of Danish artists who gathered in Faaborg around 1900. The museum itself was built to house their works, and the harmonious architecture reflects the ideals of the period. A gem of Danish art history.

museums.name: Fiskeri- og Søfartsmuseet
museums.description: Fiskeri- og Søfartsmuseet in Esbjerg is a sea aquarium and maritime museum combined. It tells the story of Denmark's fishing industry and seafaring traditions along the North Sea coast. The live seal pool is a favourite with visitors of all ages.

museums.name: Fotografisk Center
museums.description: Fotografisk Center in Copenhagen is Denmark's leading centre for photography as an art form. The programme spans documentary, fine art, and experimental photography from Denmark and around the world. An essential destination for lovers of the photographic image.

museums.name: Frederiksbergmuseerne
museums.description: Frederiksbergmuseerne encompasses several museums on Frederiksberg, covering local history and art. Collections reflect the character of this historic urban municipality that is entirely surrounded by Copenhagen. An intimate look at the culture and heritage of a unique Danish community.

museums.name: Fuglsang Kunstmuseum
museums.description: Fuglsang Kunstmuseum on Lolland holds a distinguished collection of Danish paintings, particularly from the Funen painters and the Skagen school. The newly expanded building harmoniously blends with the surrounding landscape and gardens. A peaceful museum destination in the south of Denmark.

museums.name: Furesø Museer
museums.description: Furesø Museer explores the history and culture of the Furesø area north of Copenhagen. Exhibitions cover local prehistory, natural history, and the lives of the communities around the beautiful Fure Lake. A fine local museum in scenic surroundings.

museums.name: Galleri Image
museums.description: Galleri Image in Aarhus is a dedicated gallery for contemporary photography and media art. The programme is international in scope and reflects the expanding possibilities of the photographic medium. A specialist gallery with a strong reputation in the Nordic art world.

museums.name: Gammel Estrup
museums.description: Gammel Estrup is a beautifully preserved Renaissance manor house in east Jutland, home to the Danish Manor House Museum. Exhibitions explore the lives of the nobility and the farm workers who served them over the centuries. A captivating glimpse into the social contrasts of Danish rural history.

museums.name: Gl. Holtegård
museums.description: Gl. Holtegård near Holte is a historic manor house that hosts exhibitions of Danish and international art. The beautiful setting and the quality of the exhibition programme make it a rewarding cultural destination north of Copenhagen. A charming combination of historic architecture and contemporary art.

museums.name: Glas – Museet for Glaskunst
museums.description: Glas – Museet for Glaskunst in Ebeltoft is dedicated to the art of glass, with a collection of studio glass art from Denmark and around the world. The museum celebrates the technical and creative possibilities of this ancient medium. A beautiful and distinctive specialist museum.

museums.name: Glud Museum
museums.description: Glud Museum is an open-air museum near Horsens featuring reconstructed historic buildings and traditional crafts. It offers a hands-on experience of rural life in Denmark from the 18th and 19th centuries. A charming and educational destination for families.

museums.name: Greve Museum
museums.description: Greve Museum documents the history of Greve municipality, from its prehistoric landscape to its modern development as a coastal suburb. The collection includes local archaeological finds, photographs, and everyday objects. A welcoming local museum with engaging community-focused exhibitions.

museums.name: HEART Herning Museum of Contemporary Art
museums.description: HEART Herning Museum of Contemporary Art presents international and Danish contemporary art in a striking modernist building. The collection is particularly strong in works from the 1960s to the present day. A bold and ambitious museum that puts Herning on the global art map.

museums.name: Haaning Collection
museums.description: Haaning Collection near Bagsværd is a private transport museum with a focus on vintage motorcycles and automobiles. The personally curated collection reflects a deep passion for the mechanics and design of early transportation. A specialist museum for dedicated enthusiasts.

museums.name: Hattemagerhuset
museums.description: Hattemagerhuset in Ribe is a heritage house dedicated to the craft of hat-making, one of the historic trades of this ancient city. The museum offers a rare glimpse into a nearly forgotten artisan tradition. Set in the heart of Denmark's oldest surviving city.

museums.name: Heerup Museum
museums.description: Heerup Museum in Rødovre is dedicated to the work of Henry Heerup, one of Denmark's most beloved and distinctive artists. His playful and humane works – paintings, sculptures, and graphic art – celebrate everyday life with warmth and wit. A joyful museum experience.

museums.name: Helsingør Kommunes Museer
museums.description: Helsingør Kommunes Museer manage a network of museums in Helsingør, covering maritime history, local culture, and the city's role as a gateway between Denmark and Sweden. Collections span from the Middle Ages to the 20th century. A rich museum scene in one of Denmark's most historic towns.

museums.name: Holstebro Kunstmuseum
museums.description: Holstebro Kunstmuseum is a regionally important art museum with a collection spanning Danish and international modern art. Known for its adventurous exhibition programme and strong engagement with contemporary artists. A cultural hub in west Jutland.

museums.name: Horsens Kunstmuseum
museums.description: Horsens Kunstmuseum holds a collection of Danish and international modern and contemporary art. The museum is housed in a fine historic building and runs a dynamic programme of temporary exhibitions alongside its permanent collection. A significant art museum in central Jutland.

museums.name: Industrimuseet Frederiks Værk
museums.description: Industrimuseet Frederiks Værk tells the story of one of Denmark's oldest industrial towns, built around an 18th-century cannon foundry and iron works. The museum is integrated into the historic industrial landscape itself. An extraordinary industrial heritage site unique in Scandinavia.

museums.name: Kastrupgårdsamlingen
museums.description: Kastrupgårdsamlingen near Copenhagen Airport houses an important collection of Danish ceramics and glass from the 20th century. The museum is set in a historic farmstead and offers a quiet contrast to the busy airport nearby. A specialist collection of great quality and charm.

museums.name: Kronborg Slot
museums.description: Kronborg Castle in Helsingør is one of the most important Renaissance castles in northern Europe and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Known worldwide as the setting of Shakespeare's Hamlet, it has guarded the sound between Denmark and Sweden for centuries. A magnificent and historically resonant landmark.

museums.name: Kroppedal Museum
museums.description: Kroppedal Museum explores the prehistory and astronomy history of the greater Copenhagen area. The museum is located near ancient burial mounds and the historic Rødovre Observatory. An unusual combination of archaeology and the history of science.

museums.name: KunstCentret Silkeborg Bad
museums.description: KunstCentret Silkeborg Bad is a contemporary art centre housed in a historic spa building in Silkeborg. The programme of exhibitions and events engages with both Danish and international art in an unusual and atmospheric setting. A distinctive cultural destination in Denmark's lake district.

museums.name: Kunsten Museum of Modern Art Aalborg
museums.description: Kunsten Museum of Modern Art in Aalborg is housed in a landmark building designed by Finnish architect Alvar Aalto. The collection spans Danish and international modernism through to the present day. One of the most architecturally distinguished museums in Denmark.

museums.name: Kunstforeningen GL STRAND
museums.description: Kunstforeningen GL STRAND in central Copenhagen is one of Denmark's oldest and most respected art associations. Housed in a historic building on Gammel Strand, it presents major exhibitions of Danish and international art. An important venue for serious art lovers in the heart of the capital.

museums.name: Kunsthal 44 Møen
museums.description: Kunsthal 44 Møen on the island of Møn presents contemporary art in a beautiful rural setting. The gallery's programme is eclectic and internationally connected, while being deeply rooted in the island's unique landscape and community. A surprising and rewarding art destination.

museums.name: Kunsthal Aarhus
museums.description: Kunsthal Aarhus is a major contemporary art institution presenting international and Danish art in a purpose-built venue. The ambitious exhibition programme and strong educational work make it a central institution in Aarhus's vibrant cultural life. A significant platform for contemporary art in Denmark's second city.

museums.name: Kunsthal Charlottenborg
museums.description: Kunsthal Charlottenborg in Copenhagen is one of Scandinavia's most prestigious contemporary art venues, housed in a magnificent baroque palace. The programme focuses on ambitious exhibitions of international and Danish contemporary art. A landmark institution at the heart of the Copenhagen art scene.

museums.name: Kunsthal n
museums.description: Kunsthal n in Copenhagen is a contemporary art space with a focused and adventurous programme of exhibitions. The gallery supports emerging and established artists in presenting ambitious new work. A dynamic and committed contemporary art venue.

museums.name: KØN - Gender Museum Denmark
museums.description: KØN – Gender Museum Denmark explores the history of gender, sexuality, and equality in Danish society. Through personal stories and historical artefacts, it challenges and enriches our understanding of identity. One of the most thought-provoking museums in Denmark.

museums.name: Københavns Museum
museums.description: Københavns Museum tells the story of Copenhagen across more than 800 years of history. From medieval market town to modern Scandinavian capital, the museum brings the city's layered history to life. A fascinating journey through the streets and souls of the Danish capital.

museums.name: Langelands Museum
museums.description: Langelands Museum documents the history and culture of the island of Langeland in the southern Danish archipelago. Collections range from prehistoric finds to the island's notable seafaring and agricultural traditions. A welcoming local museum in a beautiful island setting.

museums.name: Lemvig Museum
museums.description: Lemvig Museum explores the history of the Lemvig area on the western Jutland coast. Exhibitions cover local prehistory, the fishing industry, and everyday rural life through the centuries. A fine regional museum with strong community ties.

museums.name: Limfjordsmuseet
museums.description: Limfjordsmuseet in Løgstør is dedicated to the history and ecology of the Limfjord, the great waterway that cuts across northern Jutland. Exhibits explore fishing, navigation, and the unique environment of this remarkable inland sea. An engaging museum for anyone curious about Danish coastal life.

museums.name: Louisiana Museum of Modern Art
museums.description: Louisiana Museum of Modern Art is one of the world's great art museums, magnificently situated on the Øresund coast north of Copenhagen. The collection spans post-war modernism to contemporary art, with works by Picasso, Warhol, and many others. The interplay of art, architecture, and landscape is simply breathtaking.

museums.name: Læsø Museum
museums.description: Læsø Museum documents life on the remote island of Læsø in the Kattegat Sea. Known for its remarkable salt production history and distinctive seaweed-thatched houses, the museum brings this unique island culture to life. A fascinating destination off the beaten path.

museums.name: M/S Museet for Søfart
museums.description: M/S Museet for Søfart is Denmark's national maritime museum, housed in a remarkable subterranean building in the dry dock at Helsingør. It tells the story of Denmark as a seafaring nation through immersive exhibitions and a superb collection of maritime artefacts. A world-class museum by architect Bjarke Ingels.

museums.name: MAPS
museums.description: MAPS – Museum of Art, Photography and Science in Odense is a dynamic cultural venue combining visual art and photography. Located in a beautifully renovated historic building, it hosts major temporary exhibitions and an engaging permanent collection. A vibrant creative hub in the heart of Odense.

museums.name: Marstal Søfartsmuseum
museums.description: Marstal Søfartsmuseum on the island of Ærø celebrates the extraordinary seafaring tradition of this small town, once home to one of Europe's largest merchant fleets. The collection includes ship models, navigational instruments, and sailors' personal stories. A compelling maritime history from a surprising place.

museums.name: Medicinsk Museion
museums.description: Medicinsk Museion explores the history of medicine in Denmark, housed in the former Surgeons' Academy in central Copenhagen. The vast collection of medical instruments, anatomical models, and pharmaceutical objects is both fascinating and thought-provoking. One of Europe's most remarkable medical history museums.

museums.name: Middelfart Museum
museums.description: Middelfart Museum explores the history of the town and region of Middelfart, including its once-thriving porpoise hunting industry. Housed in historic merchant's premises, the museum offers a vivid portrait of small-town Danish life across the centuries. A charming and distinctive regional museum.

museums.name: Moesgaard Museum
museums.description: Moesgaard Museum near Aarhus is one of Denmark's most spectacular museums, housed in a dramatically sloping green-roofed building. Its collection covers prehistory, the Viking Age, and ancient cultures, with the world-famous Grauballe Man bog body as a highlight. An extraordinary museum experience in a stunning natural setting.

museums.name: Munkeruphus
museums.description: Munkeruphus near Dronningmølle is an intimate art venue set in a beautiful villa overlooking the sea north of Copenhagen. The programme focuses on contemporary Danish and Nordic art in an atmosphere of calm and contemplation. A peaceful and inspiring artistic retreat.

museums.name: Museerne i Brønderslev Kommune
museums.description: Museerne i Brønderslev Kommune manage a network of local museums covering the history and culture of the Brønderslev area in north Jutland. Collections explore local prehistory, agriculture, and community life through the centuries. A welcoming set of museums reflecting a proud regional identity.

museums.name: Museet for Samtidskunst
museums.description: Museet for Samtidskunst in Roskilde is dedicated to contemporary art with a strong focus on experimental and site-specific works. The museum engages actively with living artists and fosters dialogue between art and society. A progressive and thought-provoking cultural institution.

museums.name: Museet på Koldinghus
museums.description: Museet på Koldinghus is housed within the magnificent ruins of Koldinghus Castle, one of Denmark's most impressive medieval fortifications. The collection covers the castle's turbulent history and Danish royal heritage over five centuries. A dramatic and beautiful museum in an unforgettable setting.

museums.name: Museum Amager
museums.description: Museum Amager explores the history and culture of the Amager peninsula, including the fascinating communities of Dutch settlers who shaped the area. Housed in a charming historic building in Dragør, it offers an intimate window into a distinctive corner of Greater Copenhagen. A hidden gem worth discovering.

museums.name: Museum Give
museums.description: Museum Give documents the history of the Give area in central Jutland, from prehistoric times through the agricultural and industrial transformations of the modern era. A friendly local museum that keeps the stories of the local community alive. Regular temporary exhibitions add fresh perspectives.

museums.name: Museum Jorn
museums.description: Museum Jorn in Silkeborg holds the world's largest collection of works by the Danish-born international artist Asger Jorn, co-founder of the CoBrA movement. The museum also houses a significant collection of other modern and contemporary Danish and international art. An essential destination for lovers of 20th-century art.

museums.name: Museum Lolland-Falster
museums.description: Museum Lolland-Falster covers the history of the two large Danish islands of Lolland and Falster. From Viking-age finds to the dramatic story of the 1872 storm flood, the museum brings the islands' layered history to life. A comprehensive regional museum with a broad and engaging collection.

museums.name: Museum Odense
museums.description: Museum Odense manages several museums across Hans Christian Andersen's home city, including the author's birthplace and childhood home. The city's rich cultural heritage spans from the Viking age to the fairy-tale writer who became Denmark's most beloved storyteller. A must-visit for fans of Danish culture and literature.

museums.name: Museum Silkeborg
museums.description: Museum Silkeborg holds a rich collection of local history and contemporary art, as well as the famous Tollund Man, one of the world's best-preserved bog bodies. The museum tells the story of Silkeborg and the surrounding Jutland lake district. The Tollund Man alone makes this a world-class attraction.

museums.name: Museum Sydøstdanmark
museums.description: Museum Sydøstdanmark covers the history of the south-east of Zealand, from prehistoric settlements to the present day. The museum manages several sites around Næstved and presents the region's diverse cultural and natural heritage. An extensive and rewarding museum network.

museums.name: Museum Sønderjylland
museums.description: Museum Sønderjylland is a network of museums covering the culturally rich border region between Denmark and Germany. The collection reflects centuries of shifting borders, cultural exchange, and the unique identity of Sønderjylland. An important museum exploring one of Denmark's most historically complex regions.

museums.name: Museum Vestfyn
museums.description: Museum Vestfyn explores the history and culture of western Funen, a landscape of rolling hills, manor houses, and traditional Danish countryside. The museum's collections span local prehistory to the recent past. A charming museum destination in some of Denmark's most picturesque countryside.

museums.name: Museumscenter Aars
museums.description: Museumscenter Aars in north Jutland is a major heritage complex centred around rich Iron Age finds from the Himmerland area. The museum features reconstructed prehistoric environments and is located near impressive burial mounds and earthworks. A fascinating destination for lovers of Danish prehistory.

museums.name: Museumscenter Hanstholm
museums.description: Museumscenter Hanstholm explores the dramatic history of the enormous German fortifications built at Hanstholm during World War II. The preserved bunkers and gun emplacements make up one of Europe's largest military heritage complexes. A powerful and sobering reminder of the wartime occupation.

museums.name: Mønsted Kalkgruber
museums.description: Mønsted Kalkgruber near Mønsted are one of Europe's largest limestone mine systems, with over 60 kilometres of tunnels carved out over centuries. Guided tours lead visitors through the cool underground labyrinth, home to thousands of hibernating bats. A unique and atmospheric underground adventure.

museums.name: Naturama
museums.description: Naturama in Svendborg is a natural history museum with a strong visual and tactile approach to exhibiting Denmark's wildlife and natural environments. Its bold architectural design and dramatic displays make nature come alive for visitors of all ages. One of Denmark's most engaging natural history museums.

museums.name: Naturhistorisk Museum
museums.description: Naturhistorisk Museum in Aarhus is a classic natural history museum with comprehensive collections covering geology, zoology, and botany. The museum presents Denmark's natural world from the ice age to the present day. A beloved institution for school trips and curious visitors alike.

museums.name: Nikolaj Kunsthal
museums.description: Nikolaj Kunsthal in Copenhagen occupies a medieval church in the old city, creating a dramatic and atmospheric setting for contemporary art. The programme is international in scope and adventurous in character. One of Copenhagen's most distinctive and exciting art venues.

museums.name: Nivaagaards Malerisamling
museums.description: Nivaagaards Malerisamling near Nivå holds one of Denmark's finest private art collections, assembled in the early 20th century. The collection is especially strong in Dutch and Flemish Old Masters, and in Danish Golden Age painting. A peaceful and rewarding museum in beautiful garden surroundings.

museums.name: Nordjyllands Historiske Museum
museums.description: Nordjyllands Historiske Museum in Aalborg holds a comprehensive collection covering north Jutland's history from the Stone Age to the 20th century. Highlights include important Viking Age finds and a reconstructed Iron Age village. A substantial and well-presented regional history museum.

museums.name: Nordjyllands Kystmuseum
museums.description: Nordjyllands Kystmuseum in Hirtshals tells the story of the North Sea coast and its fishing communities. Exhibits explore the dangerous but vital work of North Sea fishermen and the landscapes and nature of the region. A vivid maritime museum with a raw and authentic character.

museums.name: Nordjyske Museer
museums.description: Nordjyske Museer is a network of museums covering north Jutland's rich cultural and historical heritage. From the prehistoric to the modern, the museums explore the distinct character of life in this northern corner of Denmark. A comprehensive museum organisation with multiple sites across the region.

museums.name: Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek
museums.description: Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek in Copenhagen houses one of northern Europe's greatest collections of ancient art, alongside an important French impressionist collection. Founded by Carl Jacobsen of the Carlsberg brewery, the museum is a magnificent building with a famous winter garden. An unmissable cultural experience in the Danish capital.

museums.name: Næstved Automobilmuseum
museums.description: Næstved Automobilmuseum presents a collection of vintage cars and motorcycles spanning the history of personal transport. The museum offers a nostalgic journey through the golden age of motoring. A rewarding destination for lovers of classic vehicles.

museums.name: Overgaden – Institut for Samtidskunst
museums.description: Overgaden – Institut for Samtidskunst in Copenhagen is a dedicated platform for contemporary Danish and international art. The intimate gallery in the Christianshavn neighbourhood is known for its thoughtful and politically engaged programme. A vital part of Copenhagen's contemporary art ecosystem.

museums.name: Randers Kunstmuseum
museums.description: Randers Kunstmuseum holds a wide-ranging collection of Danish and international art from the 19th century to the present. The museum actively develops its collection and presents ambitious temporary exhibitions alongside its permanent galleries. A significant art museum in the heart of Jutland.

museums.name: Ribe Kunstmuseum
museums.description: Ribe Kunstmuseum in Denmark's oldest city houses an important collection of Danish Golden Age painting and later 19th-century art. The historic building and tranquil garden add to the charm of a visit. A beautiful museum in a beautifully preserved medieval setting.

museums.name: Ringkøbing-Skjern Museum
museums.description: Ringkøbing-Skjern Museum covers the history of this coastal region in west Jutland, from the lagoon landscape of Ringkøbing Fjord to the North Sea. Collections explore local prehistory, the amber trade, and life in this distinctive natural environment. A fine regional museum with a strong sense of place.

museums.name: Roskilde Museum
museums.description: Roskilde Museum explores the history of Roskilde from its origins as a Viking trading centre and later royal seat. The city's 1,000-year history is brought to life through archaeological finds, medieval objects, and stories of the people who shaped this important Danish city. A rich and rewarding local museum.

museums.name: Rudersdal Museer
museums.description: Rudersdal Museer manages a network of local history museums north of Copenhagen. Collections cover the history of this leafy suburban municipality, with its grand old estates, artists' colonies, and natural landscapes. A series of intimate and well-presented local museums.

museums.name: SMK – Statens Museum for Kunst
museums.description: SMK – the National Gallery of Denmark in Copenhagen holds the country's greatest art collection, spanning 700 years of European and Danish art. From Rubens and Rembrandt to Matisse and the Danish Golden Age, the collection is extraordinarily rich. A world-class museum at the heart of Danish cultural life.

museums.name: Skagens Museum
museums.description: Skagens Museum holds the definitive collection of paintings by the Skagen artists, the group of Scandinavian painters who gathered at the tip of Denmark in the late 19th century. Their luminous depictions of light, sea, and community life remain some of the most beloved images in Nordic art. An essential Danish art pilgrimage.

museums.name: Skovgaard Museet
museums.description: Skovgaard Museet in Viborg is dedicated to the work of the Skovgaard family of artists, most notably Joakim Skovgaard, who painted the magnificent frescoes in Viborg Cathedral. The museum's intimate collection gives a unique insight into a dynasty of great Danish painters. A specialist gem for art lovers.

museums.name: Sommer's Automobile Museum
museums.description: Sommer's Automobile Museum near Nærum houses a private collection of carefully restored vintage and classic cars. The museum traces the development of the automobile through well-preserved examples from across the decades. An intimate and passionate celebration of automotive history.

museums.name: Sophienholm
museums.description: Sophienholm in Lyngby is a beautifully restored 18th-century manor house that functions as an art exhibition venue. Temporary exhibitions are held in the elegant rooms overlooking the lake and gardens. A refined and peaceful cultural destination north of Copenhagen.

museums.name: Sorø Kunstmuseum
museums.description: Sorø Kunstmuseum in the tranquil town of Sorø holds a focused collection of Danish art from the 19th century to the present. The museum is beautifully situated near Sorø Lake and the historic Sorø Abbey. A peaceful and rewarding art museum destination in rural Zealand.

museums.name: Struer Museum
museums.description: Struer Museum documents the history of Struer and the Limfjord area, including the town's globally significant connection to Bang & Olufsen audio design. Exhibitions cover local culture, industry, and the stories of the community. A museum that combines local pride with global reach.

museums.name: Svendborg Museum
museums.description: Svendborg Museum tells the story of Svendborg and the south Funen archipelago, one of Denmark's most beautiful coastal areas. Collections span local history, seafaring, and the remarkable island culture of the region. An engaging museum in a charming maritime town.

museums.name: Sydvestjyske Museer
museums.description: Sydvestjyske Museer cover the cultural history of south-west Jutland, including Ribe, one of Denmark's oldest and best-preserved medieval cities. The collections include important Viking Age finds and artefacts from centuries of trade along the North Sea coast. A region rich in history and well served by its museums.

museums.name: Thorvaldsens Museum
museums.description: Thorvaldsens Museum in Copenhagen is dedicated to Bertel Thorvaldsen, Denmark's greatest sculptor and one of the leading figures of European Neoclassicism. The museum houses his own extensive collection of ancient art alongside his sculptures and personal belongings. A unique and serene museum experience in the heart of the city.

museums.name: Trapholt
museums.description: Trapholt in Kolding is a leading Danish museum of modern art, design, craft, and furniture. The collection is particularly strong in mid-century Danish furniture design, with an outstanding collection of chairs. Beautifully situated on the banks of the Kolding Fjord.

museums.name: Universe Science Park
museums.description: Universe Science Park in Nordborg is a science and technology theme park with interactive exhibits exploring space, energy, and the human body. The park combines education and entertainment in an accessible outdoor and indoor setting. A lively and engaging destination for families.

museums.name: VW & Retro Museum
museums.description: VW & Retro Museum in Ulfborg is dedicated to Volkswagen vehicles and the broader culture of retro motoring. The enthusiast-run collection spans decades of VW history and related popular culture. A fun and nostalgic destination for fans of the Beetle and beyond.

museums.name: Valdemars Slot
museums.description: Valdemars Slot on the island of Tåsinge is one of Denmark's best-preserved Baroque manor houses, built for King Christian IV's son. The castle houses a museum of hunting trophies, maritime history, and royal memorabilia. A magnificent historic building in a beautiful coastal setting.

museums.name: Vardemuseerne
museums.description: Vardemuseerne is a network of museums covering the Varde area and the connected Wadden Sea coast in south-west Jutland. Museums in the network include Blåvand Museum and Tirpitz. A diverse cultural offering in a UNESCO World Heritage landscape.

museums.name: Vejen Kunstmuseum
museums.description: Vejen Kunstmuseum holds an important collection of Danish sculpture and painting from the late 19th century to the present. The museum is particularly known for its collection of works by the sculptor Niels Hansen Jacobsen. A fine regional museum punching well above its weight.

museums.name: Vendsyssel Historiske Museum
museums.description: Vendsyssel Historiske Museum in Hjørring explores the rich history and culture of the Vendsyssel region in the far north of Jutland. Collections range from prehistoric finds to modern community life. A comprehensive regional museum with a strong sense of local identity.

museums.name: Vendsyssel Kunstmuseum
museums.description: Vendsyssel Kunstmuseum in Hjørring presents Danish and international art with a focus on the 20th and 21st centuries. The museum runs an ambitious exhibition programme and manages a growing collection. A culturally vibrant institution in the northernmost part of mainland Denmark.

museums.name: Viborg Kunsthal
museums.description: Viborg Kunsthal presents contemporary art with a particular focus on the relationship between art and its social context. The programme includes international and Danish artists and engages actively with the local community. A culturally ambitious institution in central Jutland.

museums.name: Viborg Museum
museums.description: Viborg Museum explores the long and layered history of Viborg, one of Denmark's oldest cathedral cities. From Iron Age settlements to the medieval bishopric, the museum traces the story of a city that was once the most powerful in Jutland. A fascinating museum in a historically significant setting.

museums.name: Vikingeskibsmuseet
museums.description: Vikingeskibsmuseet in Roskilde is home to five original Viking ships excavated from Roskilde Fjord in 1962. Visitors can watch craftspeople build replica Viking ships using traditional techniques, and even sail on the fjord in summer. One of Denmark's most exciting and authentically immersive museums.

museums.name: Willumsens Museum
museums.description: Willumsens Museum in Frederikssund holds the largest collection of works by the visionary Danish artist J.F. Willumsen. The collection spans painting, sculpture, ceramics, and photography, reflecting a remarkably diverse artistic career. A distinctive museum dedicated to one of Denmark's most original creative minds.

museums.name: Ærø Museum
museums.description: Ærø Museum on the island of Ærø documents the maritime and cultural history of this charming island. From its seafaring heyday to its present-day reputation as a peaceful rural retreat, the museum celebrates Ærø's distinctive island character. A delightful destination in the south Danish archipelago.

museums.name: Øhavsmuseet
museums.description: Øhavsmuseet covers the cultural history of the south Funen archipelago, one of Denmark's most idyllic island landscapes. Collections explore the maritime traditions, crafts, and community life of the islands. A museum deeply rooted in the unique ecology and culture of this remarkable region.

museums.name: Østfyns Museer
museums.description: Østfyns Museer manage a network of museums covering east Funen, including the town of Nyborg, Denmark's first capital. The collections explore the history of this historically important region from the Viking age to the modern era. A rich museum network in a beautiful corner of Funen.

museums.name: Østsjællands Museum
museums.description: Østsjællands Museum in Store Heddinge explores the geology, prehistory, and local history of the Stevns peninsula on southern Zealand. The dramatic chalk cliffs of Stevns Klint, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, provide a spectacular backdrop to the museum's story. A museum where geology and human history intertwine.

museums.name: Rosenborgsamlingen
museums.description: Rosenborg Castle in central Copenhagen was built by King Christian IV in the early 17th century and houses the Danish crown jewels. The castle's sumptuous state rooms display royal treasures accumulated over centuries. One of Denmark's most visited and historically significant royal palaces.

museums.name: Statens Naturhistoriske Museum
museums.description: The Natural History Museum of Denmark in Copenhagen holds one of the world's great natural history collections. From dinosaur skeletons and meteorites to insects and botanical specimens, the museum explores the diversity of life on Earth. A classic and impressive museum housed in historic university buildings.

museums.name: Prinsens Palais
museums.description: Prinsens Palais in Copenhagen houses part of the National Museum of Denmark's collection, covering Danish cultural history from the Viking age onwards. The palace building itself is a fine example of 18th-century Danish architecture. A significant cultural institution in the heart of the capital.

museums.name: Science Museerne
museums.description: Science Museerne in Aarhus encompasses several science and natural history museums, including Steno Museum and Ole Rømer Observatory. The museums explore the history of science, medicine, and astronomy in an engaging and accessible way. A rich destination for curious minds in Aarhus.

museums.name: Museum Østjylland Ebeltoft
museums.description: Museum Østjylland in Ebeltoft explores the history and culture of the Ebeltoft area on the Jutland coast. The beautiful old town of Ebeltoft provides a historic setting for exhibitions covering local trade, seafaring, and community life. A charming museum in one of Denmark's best-preserved old towns.

museums.name: H.C. Andersens Hus
museums.description: H.C. Andersens Hus in Odense is the birthplace museum dedicated to Denmark's most famous author, Hans Christian Andersen. The new building, opened in 2021, immerses visitors in the fairy-tale world of the author through innovative design and storytelling. A magical experience for children and adults alike.

museums.name: Frilandsmuseet
museums.description: Frilandsmuseet near Copenhagen is one of the world's largest open-air museums, with over 50 historic buildings relocated from across Denmark. Costumed guides and live demonstrations recreate rural and urban life from the 17th to the early 20th century. A wonderful and spacious outdoor museum experience.

museums.name: Danmarks Borgcenter
museums.description: Danmarks Borgcenter in Vordingborg is dedicated to the history of Danish castles and medieval fortifications. Situated in the ruins of Vordingborg Castle, it explores the political and military power of the Danish kings in the Middle Ages. A dramatic museum in an evocative historic setting.

museums.name: Amalienborg Museet Christian VIIIs Palæ
museums.description: Amalienborg Museet in Copenhagen presents the history of the Danish royal family from 1863 to the present day. The museum occupies rooms in Christian VIII's Palace, one of the four palaces surrounding Amalienborg Square. An intimate look at royal life in the world's oldest monarchy.

museums.name: VejleMuseerne
museums.description: VejleMuseerne is a network of museums covering the Vejle area and the historically significant Jutland landscape. The region is home to remarkable Iron Age finds including the famous Haraldskær Woman. An important museum network in a city known for both history and modern architecture.

museums.name: Rudolph Tegners Museum
museums.description: Rudolph Tegners Museum near Dronningmølle houses an extraordinary collection of sculptures by the Danish artist Rudolph Tegner. The dramatic purpose-built museum, designed by Tegner himself, stands on a hilltop overlooking the sea. A unique and powerfully atmospheric museum experience.

museums.name: Cisternerne
museums.description: Cisternerne beneath Frederiksberg Hill is a former underground reservoir turned art and exhibition space. The vast vaulted chambers drip with stalactites and offer a hauntingly beautiful backdrop for contemporary art installations. One of Copenhagen's most atmospheric and surprising venues.

museums.name: Ordrupgaard
museums.description: Ordrupgaard north of Copenhagen holds a world-class collection of French Impressionist and Post-Impressionist art alongside Danish Golden Age painting. The museum buildings include a historic villa and a bold extension by Zaha Hadid. A beautiful museum with a garden, café, and one of Denmark's finest art collections.

museums.name: Den Fynske Landsby
museums.description: Den Fynske Landsby is an open-air museum near Odense recreating 19th-century village life on Funen. The collection of historic farm buildings and crafts workshops gives visitors a vivid sense of Danish rural life in the age of Hans Christian Andersen. A charming and educational outdoor experience.

museums.name: Det Grønne Museum
museums.description: Det Grønne Museum in Auning explores the history of Danish agriculture and the rural landscape. Housed on a historic estate, it celebrates the farming traditions that shaped the Danish countryside and fed the nation. A fine outdoor and indoor museum destination in east Jutland.

museums.name: Fængselsmuseet
museums.description: Fængselsmuseet in Horsens occupies a former Victorian prison and tells the story of Danish crime and punishment across two centuries. Visitors can explore the original cells, courtrooms, and isolation ward in a genuinely atmospheric setting. A gripping and thought-provoking museum experience.

museums.name: Krigsmuseet
museums.description: Krigsmuseet in Copenhagen is Denmark's national military museum, covering 500 years of Danish military history. The collection includes weapons, uniforms, medals, and personal stories from conflicts spanning from the Renaissance to modern times. A comprehensive and well-presented military history museum.

museums.name: Holmegaard Værk
museums.description: Holmegaard Værk in Fensmark celebrates the art of Danish glassmaking, rooted in the traditions of the historic Holmegaard Glassworks. The museum traces the evolution of Danish glass design from functional utility to iconic mid-century modernism. A beautiful specialist museum with exceptional craftsmanship on display.

museums.name: Hjerl Hedes Frilandsmuseum
museums.description: Hjerl Hedes Frilandsmuseum in Vinderup is a large open-air museum set in a heathland landscape in western Jutland. Historic buildings have been relocated from across Denmark to recreate village and farm life from the Stone Age to the 1900s. One of Denmark's largest and most atmospheric outdoor museums.

museums.name: Den Hirschsprungske Samling
museums.description: Den Hirschsprungske Samling in Copenhagen holds one of Denmark's finest collections of 19th-century Danish painting. Assembled by tobacco magnate Heinrich Hirschsprung, the collection is particularly rich in works by the Skagen artists and the Funen painters. An intimate and beautifully curated museum in the heart of the city.

museums.name: Frihedsmuseet
museums.description: Frihedsmuseet – the Museum of Danish Resistance – in Copenhagen tells the story of Denmark under German occupation from 1940 to 1945. Through personal testimonies and artefacts, it documents both the courage of the resistance and the moral complexity of everyday life under occupation. A deeply important and moving national museum.

museums.name: Sønderborg Slot
museums.description: Sønderborg Slot is a magnificent castle situated on the shores of the Als Sound in southern Jutland. The museum covers the turbulent history of the border region between Denmark and Germany, from the Middle Ages through to the reunification of 1920. A dramatic historic site with a powerful story to tell.

museums.name: NaturBornholm
museums.description: NaturBornholm in Aakirkeby explores the unique geology and natural history of the island of Bornholm. The island's ancient granite landscape, visible through the museum's spectacular views, has shaped both its ecology and its human history. An inspiring destination for those curious about the natural world.

museums.name: H.C. Andersens Barndomshjem
museums.description: H.C. Andersens Barndomshjem is the modest Odense house where Hans Christian Andersen spent much of his childhood. The intimate rooms give a poignant sense of the humble origins of the author who would become a global literary icon. A touching complement to the larger H.C. Andersens Hus nearby.

museums.name: Johannes Larsen Museet
museums.description: Johannes Larsen Museet in Kerteminde is dedicated to the work of painter Johannes Larsen, one of the Funen artists who celebrated the natural world. The museum includes the artist's beautifully preserved home and garden by the sea. A serene and inspirational museum experience.

museums.name: Karen Blixen-Museet Rungstedlund
museums.description: Karen Blixen-Museet Rungstedlund is the former home of author Karen Blixen, best known internationally for Out of Africa. The house, gardens, and surrounding bird sanctuary are preserved as a memorial to her life and remarkable literary legacy. A tranquil and deeply personal museum experience.

museums.name: Danmarks Rockmuseum
museums.description: Danmarks Rockmuseum in Roskilde celebrates Denmark's rock and pop music heritage from the 1950s to the present day. Interactive exhibits let visitors experience the history of Danish music in an entertaining and immersive way. A lively museum for music lovers of all generations.

museums.name: Kystmuseet Bangsbo Fort
museums.description: Kystmuseet Bangsbo Fort in Frederikshavn explores the history of the Danish coastline and the military fortifications that defended it. The restored Bangsbo Fort, used during World War II, provides a dramatic and evocative setting. A compelling museum on the edge of the North Sea.

museums.name: Kystmuseet Skagen
museums.description: Kystmuseet Skagen tells the story of life at the very tip of Denmark, where the Skagerrak and Kattegat seas meet. Exhibitions cover fishing, seafaring, and the natural phenomena of the shifting sands that have buried entire villages. A fascinating coastal museum in one of Denmark's most dramatic landscapes.

museums.name: Museum Østjylland Randers
museums.description: Museum Østjylland in Randers covers the history of the Randers area from its Viking Age origins to the present day. The museum manages multiple sites across the region and presents a broad panorama of east Jutland's cultural heritage. A substantial regional museum with engaging and varied collections.

museums.name: Museet Ribes Vikinger
museums.description: Museet Ribes Vikinger brings the Viking Age to life in Denmark's oldest surviving city. The museum uses reconstructions, artefacts, and immersive storytelling to recreate life in Ribe during the 8th and 9th centuries, when it was one of northern Europe's most important trading centres. A world-class Viking museum.

museums.name: Middelaldercenteret Lolland-Falster
museums.description: Middelaldercenteret Lolland-Falster is a living history museum dedicated to medieval life in southern Denmark. Visitors can watch craftspeople demonstrate medieval skills, explore reconstructed buildings, and experience the rhythm of life in the Middle Ages. A hands-on and entertaining museum experience.

museums.name: Vikingemuseet Ladby
museums.description: Vikingemuseet Ladby preserves one of the world's most extraordinary Viking Age finds – a ship burial containing the remains of a Viking chieftain, his horses, and his dogs. The original burial mound covers the actual excavation site. A unique and haunting window into Viking beliefs about death and the afterlife.

museums.name: Aalborg Søfarts- og Marinemuseum Springer
museums.description: Aalborg Søfarts- og Marinemuseum Springer in Aalborg features a real Cold War submarine as its centrepiece. The museum explores Danish naval and maritime history with the U-359 submarine as its most dramatic exhibit. A gripping and atmospheric museum destination on the Limfjord waterfront.

museums.name: KØS Museum for Kunst i det Offentlige Rum
museums.description: KØS Museum for Kunst i det Offentlige Rum in Køge is dedicated to art in public spaces. The collection spans Danish and international works created for public environments, from monumental sculptures to urban installations. A thought-provoking museum that connects art with everyday life.

museums.name: Koldkrigsmuseum Stevnsfort
museums.description: Koldkrigsmuseum Stevnsfort explores the Cold War history of the Stevns peninsula, a strategically vital NATO listening post during the Cold War. The underground fortress, carved into the chalk cliffs, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. A fascinating and slightly eerie window into a tense period of history.

museums.name: ENIGMA Museum for post tele og kommunikation
museums.description: ENIGMA Museum for post, tele og kommunikation in Copenhagen is a museum of communication history, from the first postal services to the digital age. Interactive and visually engaging, it explores how communication technology has transformed Danish society. A stimulating museum for all ages.

museums.name: Industrimuseet Horsens
museums.description: Industrimuseet Horsens explores the industrial heritage of Horsens, from its 19th-century manufacturing roots to the present. Located in a beautifully restored factory building, the museum tells the story of work, technology, and community. A rich and evocative industrial history museum.

museums.name: Strandingsmuseum St George
museums.description: Strandingsmuseum St George on the west Jutland coast tells the story of shipwrecks along this treacherous coastline. The museum takes its name from the British warship St George, wrecked in 1811 with the loss of over 900 lives. A gripping maritime museum on one of Europe's most dangerous coasts.

museums.name: Køge Museum
museums.description: Køge Museum explores the history of Køge, one of Denmark's best-preserved Renaissance merchant towns. Collections cover local archaeology, the town's commercial history, and the stories of the people who lived there. A rewarding museum in a beautifully historic setting.

museums.name: Vikingemuseet Lindholm Høje
museums.description: Vikingemuseet Lindholm Høje is one of Scandinavia's most important Viking Age burial sites, with over 700 graves marked by stone ships. The adjacent museum presents finds from the site and explores Viking Age burial customs. A deeply atmospheric place where ancient history feels very close.

museums.name: Vikingemuseet Fyrkat
museums.description: Vikingemuseet Fyrkat near Hobro is built around a perfectly preserved Viking ring fortress dating from around 980 AD. The reconstructed longhouses give a vivid sense of how Harald Bluetooth's military power was projected across Denmark. A unique and accessible Viking heritage site.

museums.name: Frøslevlejrens Museum
museums.description: Frøslevlejrens Museum in Padborg commemorates the Danish internment camp where thousands of Danes were imprisoned by German occupiers during World War II. The preserved camp buildings provide a sobering and authentic memorial. A profoundly moving museum at the border between Denmark and Germany.

museums.name: Landbrugsmuseet Melstedgård
museums.description: Landbrugsmuseet Melstedgård on Bornholm is an open-air museum set on a historic farm, exploring traditional Bornholm agricultural life. The well-preserved buildings and their contents give a rich picture of island farming in the 18th and 19th centuries. A peaceful and beautifully situated rural museum.

museums.name: Museum Mors
museums.description: Museum Mors on the island of Mors in the Limfjord explores the distinctive natural and cultural heritage of this island community. Known for its unique geology and the diatomite quarries that shaped its economy, Mors has a fascinating story. A fine local museum with great natural history collections.

museums.name: Graabrødrekloster Museum
museums.description: Graabrødrekloster Museum in Odense is set in the ruins of a medieval Franciscan friary, one of the best-preserved in Denmark. Excavations have revealed centuries of monastic life, and the atmospheric site offers a powerful connection to the medieval city. A hidden gem in the heart of Odense.

museums.name: Øregaard Museum
museums.description: Øregaard Museum in Hellerup is a historic villa housing a collection of Danish art from the late 19th and early 20th centuries. The intimate setting and beautiful garden create a peaceful atmosphere for appreciating Danish painting from this productive period. A refined and charming museum.

museums.name: Storm P Museet
museums.description: Storm P Museet in Frederiksberg is dedicated to the beloved Danish cartoonist Robert Storm Petersen, known as Storm P. His witty and warmly humane cartoons and philosophical one-liners remain beloved in Denmark. The museum celebrates a uniquely Danish sense of humour.

museums.name: Geomuseum Faxe
museums.description: Geomuseum Faxe explores the remarkable geology of the Faxe area, where a coral reef from 66 million years ago is preserved in a working quarry. The museum explains the geological story of Denmark and the ancient sea creatures whose fossils can still be found at the site. A remarkable natural history destination.

museums.name: Kunstmuseet i Tønder
museums.description: Kunstmuseet i Tønder holds a distinguished collection of Danish and international art from the 20th century. Located in the historic border town of Tønder, the museum reflects the cultural richness of this bicultural region. A welcoming and well-curated regional art museum.

museums.name: Ballerup Museum
museums.description: Ballerup Museum explores the history of Ballerup municipality, tracing the transformation of this area from a rural farming district to a modern Copenhagen suburb. The collections cover archaeology, local industry, and everyday community life. A fine local history museum.

museums.name: Fur Museum
museums.description: Fur Museum is dedicated to the extraordinary geology of the island of Fur in the Limfjord, famous for its deposits of diatomite and the fossils preserved within it. The museum's collection of 55-million-year-old fossils, including birds and fish, is internationally significant. A wonderful natural history treasure.

museums.name: Museum Ovartaci
museums.description: Museum Ovartaci in Aarhus holds a unique collection of art created by patients at the Psychiatric Hospital Risskov over the course of the 20th century. The works offer a powerful and often beautiful window into extraordinary inner worlds. One of Denmark's most unusual and thought-provoking museum experiences.

museums.name: Kalundborg Museum
museums.description: Kalundborg Museum explores the history of Kalundborg, dominated by the remarkable five-towered Kalundborg Church, one of Denmark's most distinctive medieval buildings. Collections cover the town's history from Viking times and its role as a significant medieval trading centre. A rich local museum in a historically fascinating setting.

museums.name: Skanderborg Museum
museums.description: Skanderborg Museum covers the history of the Skanderborg area, from prehistoric settlements to the royal residence that once stood there. The museum explores how this part of Jutland's lake district was shaped by nature, agriculture, and royal power. A well-presented regional museum in beautiful surroundings.

museums.name: Museerne i Fredericia
museums.description: Museerne i Fredericia manage the cultural heritage of this planned fortress city in central Jutland. The collections cover the city's military origins, the famous battle of 1849, and the diverse communities who have called Fredericia home. A museum network with a strong and distinctive local story.

museums.name: Skive Museum
museums.description: Skive Museum explores the history and culture of the Skive area and the surrounding Limfjord landscape. Collections cover local prehistory, the fishing and farming traditions of the region, and the stories of its communities. A welcoming regional museum in a characterful market town.

museums.name: Sorø Museum
museums.description: Sorø Museum documents the history of Sorø, a small town with a disproportionately large cultural footprint as the seat of the historic Sorø Academy. Collections cover the town's medieval monastery, its noble academy, and local community life. A fine local museum in a historically rich setting.

museums.name: Stiftsmuseet Maribo
museums.description: Stiftsmuseet Maribo is located in the historic cathedral city of Maribo on Lolland. The museum covers the religious history of the area, particularly the Birgittine monastery that was one of the most important in medieval Scandinavia. A specialist museum of considerable historical depth.

museums.name: Aalborg Historiske Museum
museums.description: Aalborg Historiske Museum holds a broad collection covering the history of Aalborg from the Viking age to the 20th century. The museum presents the story of this north Jutland city, once one of Denmark's most important commercial centres. A substantial and well-presented urban history museum.

museums.name: Lejre Museum
museums.description: Lejre Museum explores the fascinating history and prehistory of the Lejre area on Zealand. The region was a seat of legendary Danish kings and holds enormous importance in the mythology and early history of Denmark. A museum that connects the deep past to living landscape.

museums.name: Ringkøbing Museum
museums.description: Ringkøbing Museum documents the history of the town of Ringkøbing and the unique landscape of the lagoon and coast. The town's history as a trading port cut off from the sea by shifting sands provides a compelling historical narrative. A characterful local museum in a picturesque west Jutland town.

museums.name: Herningsholm Museum
museums.description: Herningsholm Museum is set in a historic half-timbered manor house in Herning, exploring the history of the estate and the surrounding textile industry that made the town famous. The beautifully restored building is itself a highlight of the visit. A charming museum with a distinctive local story.

museums.name: Carl Nielsen Museet
museums.description: Carl Nielsen Museet in Odense celebrates the life and music of Denmark's greatest composer, Carl Nielsen. The museum explores his remarkable journey from a poor Funen farm boy to one of the defining voices of European classical music. An inspiring museum for music lovers.

museums.name: Tekstilmuseet
museums.description: Tekstilmuseet in Herning is dedicated to the history of the Danish textile and fashion industry, which was centred in Herning. From early weaving to the global fashion brands the city has produced, the museum tells a surprising and engaging story. A specialist museum in Denmark's textile capital.

museums.name: Esbjerg Museum
museums.description: Esbjerg Museum documents the history of Esbjerg, a city that was founded only in 1868 but grew rapidly into Denmark's most important North Sea port. Collections cover the city's development, its fishing industry, and its role as a gateway to the wider world. A lively urban history museum.

museums.name: Øm Kloster Museum
museums.description: Øm Kloster Museum near Ry explores the history of Øm Abbey, a Cistercian monastery founded in 1172. Excavations have revealed a rich collection of medieval artefacts, and the atmospheric lakeside site adds to the sense of history. A moving and beautiful medieval heritage site.

museums.name: Vesthimmerlands Museum
museums.description: Vesthimmerlands Museum covers the history and prehistory of the Himmerland region in north Jutland. The area is rich in Iron Age and Bronze Age remains, and the museum presents these finds alongside the story of later rural and industrial life. A fine regional museum with strong archaeological collections.

museums.name: Reventlow-Museet
museums.description: Reventlow-Museet is dedicated to Count Christian Ditlev Frederik Reventlow, one of the architects of the great agricultural reform that transformed Denmark in the late 18th century. Set in the historic estate where his work began, the museum explores one of the most important chapters in Danish social history.

museums.name: Horsens Historiske Museum
museums.description: Horsens Historiske Museum explores the long history of Horsens from its medieval origins to the present. The town's intriguing past, including its role as a major prison city, is brought to life through rich collections and engaging exhibitions. A substantial local history museum.

museums.name: Frederikssund Museum Færgegården
museums.description: Frederikssund Museum Færgegården is set in a historic ferry inn on the Roskilde Fjord, telling the story of the old ferry crossing and local life along the shore. The beautifully preserved building and its waterside location make for a very atmospheric visit. A charming local history museum.

museums.name: Hals Museum Hals Skanse
museums.description: Hals Museum Hals Skanse explores the history of the fortification at Hals, which controlled the eastern entrance to the Limfjord for centuries. The restored earthworks and the adjacent museum tell the story of this strategically vital location. A fascinating military heritage site on the edge of the sea.

museums.name: Odsherreds Museum
museums.description: Odsherreds Museum covers the history of the Odsherred peninsula in north-west Zealand, a landscape of ancient burial mounds, fjords, and forests. Collections explore prehistory, local crafts, and the summer colony of artists who discovered the area in the early 20th century. A fine regional museum in a beautiful landscape.

museums.name: Odsherreds Kunstmuseum Hvide Hus
museums.description: Odsherreds Kunstmuseum Hvide Hus is dedicated to the Danish artist colony that gathered at Odsherred in the early 20th century. The museum presents works by the painters who were captivated by the area's light and landscape. A specialist art museum in a scenic and historically significant artistic destination.

museums.name: Tycho Brahe Planetarium
museums.description: Tycho Brahe Planetarium in Copenhagen is Denmark's national planetarium, named after the famous 16th-century Danish astronomer. The dome cinema shows spectacular films about space and the cosmos, and the museum explores the history of astronomy. A dazzling and educational experience in the heart of Copenhagen.

museums.name: Fregatten Jylland
museums.description: Fregatten Jylland in Ebeltoft is the world's longest preserved wooden warship, launched in 1860. The fully restored frigate allows visitors to explore every deck and compartment of this magnificent vessel. An extraordinary piece of Danish naval history afloat in Ebeltoft harbour.

museums.name: Politimuseet
museums.description: Politimuseet in Copenhagen documents the history of the Danish police from its earliest origins to the present day. Collections include uniforms, equipment, and artefacts relating to famous criminal cases. A fascinating specialist museum for those interested in law and order.

museums.name: Bornholms Middelaldercenter
museums.description: Bornholms Middelaldercenter in Gudhjem is a living history museum recreating medieval life on Bornholm. Craftspeople demonstrate traditional skills, and the reconstructed buildings give a vivid sense of the island's medieval character. A hands-on and educational museum experience.

museums.name: Danmarks Flymuseum
museums.description: Danmarks Flymuseum in Stauning is the national aviation museum of Denmark, with a comprehensive collection of historic aircraft spanning the history of Danish aviation. Visitors can explore aircraft from the early days of flight to the jet age. A spectacular museum for aviation enthusiasts.

museums.name: Cirkusmuseet
museums.description: Cirkusmuseet in Hvidovre is dedicated to the history of the circus in Denmark, with a colourful collection of costumes, posters, props, and memorabilia. The museum celebrates the acrobats, clowns, and animals that brought magic to Danish audiences for over two centuries. A joyful and entertaining specialist museum.

museums.name: Sea War Museum Jutland
museums.description: Sea War Museum Jutland in Thyborøn explores the history of the Battle of Jutland, the largest naval battle of World War I. The museum presents the dramatic events of May 1916 through artefacts recovered from the seabed and immersive storytelling. A compelling military history museum on the edge of the North Sea.

museums.name: Søby Brunkulsmuseum
museums.description: Søby Brunkulsmuseum in Ikast documents the history of the brown coal industry that was vital to Denmark's energy supply during World War II. The museum preserves the machinery, stories, and landscape of this remarkable industrial chapter. A fascinating specialist industrial heritage museum.

museums.name: Papirmuseet Bikuben
museums.description: Papirmuseet Bikuben in Silkeborg is a museum dedicated to the art of paper and the history of papermaking. Located in a historic paper mill, it explores the craft traditions and industrial history of this essential material. A fine specialist museum in a beautiful riverside setting.

museums.name: Oluf Høst Museet
museums.description: Oluf Høst Museet in Gudhjem on Bornholm is dedicated to the work of painter Oluf Høst, one of the most celebrated artists associated with the island. His bold and colourful depictions of Bornholm's landscape and light are displayed in his own restored farmhouse. A beautiful and intimate artist's museum.

museums.name: Hjorths Fabrik Bornholms Keramikmuseum
museums.description: Hjorths Fabrik Bornholms Keramikmuseum in Rønne celebrates the long tradition of ceramics production on Bornholm. The museum is housed in a historic pottery factory and presents both the industrial and artistic history of Bornholm ceramics. A fine specialist museum celebrating a distinctive island craft.

museums.name: Museet for Religiøs Kunst
museums.description: Museet for Religiøs Kunst in Lemvig holds a collection of religious art spanning centuries of Christian devotion in Denmark. The museum explores the role of art in religious life and the extraordinary craftsmanship devoted to spiritual expression. A thoughtful and contemplative museum experience.

museums.name: Jacob A Riis Museum
museums.description: Jacob A Riis Museum in Ribe honours the Danish-American journalist and social reformer Jacob Riis, who documented poverty in New York's slums in the late 19th century. The museum explores his remarkable life and the powerful impact of his photographs. An inspiring museum about a man who used images to change the world.

museums.name: Museet Herregården Hessel
museums.description: Museet Herregården Hessel near Hadsund is set in a beautifully preserved manor house in north Jutland. The museum explores the history of Danish manor house culture and the lives of those who lived and worked on the estate. A charming and historically rich destination.

museums.name: Nymindegab Museum
museums.description: Nymindegab Museum documents the history of this tiny settlement on the west Jutland coast, where fishermen lived in extraordinary isolation at the edge of the North Sea. The museum captures the raw and resilient character of life on the Danish coastline. An evocative and atmospheric local museum.

museums.name: Gasmuseet
museums.description: Gasmuseet in Hobro is dedicated to the history of the Danish gas industry, from town gas production to modern natural gas. Housed in a historic gasworks, the museum explores how gas transformed Danish homes and industry. A specialist museum with a surprisingly engaging story to tell.

museums.name: Fyrhistorisk Museum
museums.description: Fyrhistorisk Museum in Frederiksværk is devoted to the history of lighthouses and lighthouse keepers along the Danish coast. The collection of lighthouse equipment, personal stories, and navigational history illuminates a vital but often forgotten service. A charming and evocative specialist museum.

museums.name: Tadre Mølle Museum
museums.description: Tadre Mølle Museum near Hvalsø is a beautifully preserved working watermill, one of the few remaining in Denmark. The museum demonstrates traditional milling techniques and explores the role of mills in rural Danish life. A peaceful and picturesque heritage destination.

museums.name: Klosterlund Museum
museums.description: Klosterlund Museum near Engesvang is set in a landscape of heather moorland and ancient peat bogs in central Jutland. The museum explores the natural history and cultural heritage of the Jutland heath, including the peat cutting industry that shaped it. A peaceful and evocative destination in a distinctive landscape.

museums.name: Herregårdsmuseet Selsø Slot
museums.description: Herregårdsmuseet Selsø Slot near Skibby is a beautifully preserved Baroque manor house on the shores of Roskilde Fjord. The museum explores the history of the estate and the aristocratic families who lived there. A serene and atmospheric historic house museum.

museums.name: Danmarks Kostumarium
museums.description: Danmarks Kostumarium in Herning holds one of Denmark's largest collections of historic costumes and textiles. The museum explores the history of Danish fashion and dress, from folk costume to haute couture. A specialist museum in Denmark's textile capital.

museums.name: Hørvævsmuseet på Krengerup
museums.description: Hørvævsmuseet på Krengerup near Glamsbjerg is dedicated to the traditional craft of linen weaving on Funen. The museum preserves the tools, techniques, and products of this once-widespread rural industry. A quiet and authentic specialist museum celebrating a disappearing craft.

museums.name: Dansk Sygeplejehistorisk Museum
museums.openingHoursEn: Tuesday–Friday 13–16, first Sunday of the month 13–16
museums.description: Dansk Sygeplejehistorisk Museum in Copenhagen documents the history of nursing in Denmark from its origins to the present day. The museum honours the women and men who have cared for the sick and explores how nursing has evolved as a profession. A respectful and enlightening specialist museum.

museums.name: Tirpitz
museums.description: Tirpitz near Blåvand is a bold new museum embedded in the sand dunes alongside a massive German World War II bunker. The sleek underground building, designed by Bjarke Ingels Group, houses exhibitions about the bunker, the amber coast, and the Wadden Sea. A stunning fusion of architecture, history, and landscape.

museums.name: Sagnlandet Lejre
museums.description: Sagnlandet Lejre is an experimental living history centre where researchers and visitors explore life in ancient Denmark. From the Stone Age to the Viking Age, the site recreates historic environments using authentic materials and techniques. One of Denmark's most fascinating and immersive historical experiences.

museums.name: Kongernes Jelling
museums.description: Kongernes Jelling is Denmark's most important Viking Age monument, where Harald Bluetooth erected the great rune stones that announced the Christianisation of Denmark. The UNESCO World Heritage Site includes burial mounds, runic stones, and a modern visitor centre. A profound and moving encounter with the birth of the Danish nation.

museums.name: Geocenter Møns Klint
museums.description: Geocenter Møns Klint is dramatically situated at the edge of the spectacular chalk cliffs of Møns Klint on the island of Møn. The museum explores the geology of the cliffs, the 70-million-year-old fossils found there, and the UNESCO Starlight Reserve that makes this one of Denmark's darkest skies. A breathtaking natural and cultural destination.

museums.name: Vadehavscentret
museums.description: Vadehavscentret in Ribe is the gateway to the UNESCO World Heritage Wadden Sea, the world's largest tidal flat system. The museum explains the extraordinary ecology of this constantly changing landscape and the migratory birds that depend on it. An inspiring introduction to one of nature's most remarkable environments.

museums.name: Ribe VikingeCenter
museums.description: Ribe VikingeCenter is one of Scandinavia's finest living history museums, recreating life in the Viking Age town of Ribe. Costumed craftspeople demonstrate ancient skills, and the site includes both museum exhibitions and reconstructed Viking environments. An immersive and educational experience for all ages.

museums.name: L.A. Rings Atelier
museums.description: L.A. Rings Atelier preserves the studio and home of the celebrated Danish Symbolist painter Laurits Andersen Ring (1854–1933), one of Denmark's most beloved 19th-century artists. The intimate setting offers visitors a glimpse into his working life and includes original works, tools, and personal effects. It is managed as part of Museum Sydøstdanmark.

museums.name: Museum Obscurum
museums.description: Museum Obskurium is an unusual cabinet of curiosities showcasing rare, strange, and thought-provoking objects from history, science, and popular culture. The museum offers an eclectic and surprising journey through the obscure and the extraordinary. A one-of-a-kind experience that appeals to the curious-minded visitor.

museums.name: Richard Winthers Hus
museums.description: Richard Winthers Hus is the preserved home and studio of the visionary Danish artist Richard Winther (1926–2007), known for his richly symbolic and intensely personal body of work. The house in the Thy landscape has been kept as it was during his lifetime, offering an authentic encounter with the artist's universe. It stands as a tribute to one of Denmark's most distinctive post-war painters.

museums.name: Det Nationalhistoriske Museum
museums.description: Det Nationalhistoriske Museum at Frederiksborg Castle is Denmark's foremost museum of national history, housed in one of the country's most magnificent Renaissance palaces. The collection spans 500 years of Danish history through portraits, furniture, and decorative arts, including the renowned Danmarks Nationalportrætmaleri. The castle and its gardens are themselves among the finest historic sites in Scandinavia.

museums.name: Det Sorte Geomuseum
museums.description: Det Sorte Geomuseum explores the dramatic geology of Stevns Klint, a UNESCO World Heritage Site famous for its dark 'fish clay' layer marking the mass extinction event 66 million years ago. The museum presents the geological and paleontological significance of the cliff face in an accessible and visually compelling way. It offers guided tours to the cliff, one of the world's clearest records of the K-Pg boundary event.

museums.name: Evolutionsmuseet
museums.description: Evolutionsmuseet at the University of Copenhagen brings together the university's historic natural history collections under one roof, spanning zoology, botany, geology, and paleontology. The museum traces the history of life on Earth through vast scientific collections and engaging exhibitions. It is one of Scandinavia's most important natural history institutions, with specimens dating back centuries.
